title: jacobaea vulgaris subsp. vulgaris
intro: Jacobaea vulgaris subsp. vulgaris, also known as common ragwort, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities, which can help to soothe sore throats and relieve respiratory issues. The health benefits of common ragwort include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of common ragwort can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
